contentBlocks.original:
न कर्मणा न प्रजया धनेन
त्यागेनैके अमृतत्वमानशुः ।
परेण नाकं निहितं गुहायां
विभ्राजते तद्यतयो विशन्ति ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na karmaṇā na prajayā dhanena
tyāgenaike amṛtatvamānaśuḥ
pareṇa nākaṃ nihitaṃ guhāyāṃ
vibhrājate tadyatayo viśanti.
contentBlocks.translation: Not by work, nor by progeny or by wealth, but by renunciation alone have some attained immortality. That (immortality) which is even beyond the heaven, is attained by the self-controlled renunciates (as the Self) shining in their heart.
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; कर्मणा (karmaṇā) – by work; न (na) – not; प्रजया (prajayā) – by progeny; धनेन (dhanena) – by wealth; त्यागेन (tyāgena) – by renunciation; एके (eke) – some; अमृतत्वम् (amṛtatvam) – immortality; आनशुः (ānaśuḥ) – attained; परेण (pareṇa) – beyond; नाकम् (nākam) – heaven; निहितम् (nihitam) – placed; गुहायाम् (guhāyām) – in the cave; विभ्राजते (vibhrājate) – shines; तत् (tat) – that; यतयः (yatayaḥ) – renunciates; विशन्ति (viśanti) – enter;

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ वेदान्तविज्ञानसुनिश्चितार्थाः
सन्यासयोगाद्यतयः शुद्धसत्त्वाः ।
ते ब्रह्मलोके तु परान्तकाले
परामृतात् परिमुच्यन्ति सर्वे ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vedantavijnanasuniścitārthah
sanyásayogadyatayah śuddhasattväh
te brahmaloke tu parantakale
paramṛtāt parimucyanti sarve.
contentBlocks.translation: Those who make proper effort and whose minds are pure, who have well-ascertained, clear knowledge of Vedanta, because of a life of sannyasa, they, at the time of the resolution of the individuality in Brahman, are all released from (even) the unmanifest (karma).
contentBlocks.meanings: वेदान्तविज्ञानसुनिश्चितार्थाः (vedāntavijñānasuniścitārthāḥ) – those with well-ascertained Vedanta knowledge (vedānta:Vedanta | vijñāna:knowledge | suniścita:well-ascertained | arthāḥ:those whose purpose); सन्यासयोगाद्यतयः (sannyāsayogādyatayaḥ) – ascetics through renunciation and yoga (sannyāsa:renunciation | yoga:yoga | yatayaḥ:ascetics); शुद्धसत्त्वाः (śuddhasattvāḥ) – pure-minded (śuddha:pure | sattvāḥ:minded); ते (te) – they; ब्रह्मलोके (brahmaloke) – in the world of Brahman (brahma:Brahman | loke:in the world); तु (tu) – but; परान्तकाले (parāntakāle) – at the end time (parānta:end | kāle:time); परामृतात् (parāmṛtāt) – from the supreme nectar (para:supreme | amṛtāt:from nectar); परिमुच्यन्ति (parimucyanti) – are released; सर्वे (sarve) – all;

contentBlocks.original:
दहं विपापं परमेश्मभूतं
यत्पुण्डरीकं पुरमध्यसग्म्स्थम्।
तत्रापि दहं गगनं विशोक
तस्मिन् यदन्तस्तदुपासितव्यम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dahram vipapam parameśmabhūtam
yatpundarīkam puramadhyasagmstham
tatrapi dahram gaganam visokas
tasmin yadantastadupäsitavyam.
contentBlocks.translation: That, which abides in the city of the body, in the lotus, which is the intellect, and in that small space, that, which is free of all hurt and guilt, free from sorrow, and of the nature of Paramesvara, should be meditated upon.
contentBlocks.meanings: दहं (dahaṃ) – small; विपापं (vipāpam) – free of guilt; परमेश्मभूतं (parameśmabhūtam) – of the nature of Paramesvara (parama:supreme | īśma:lord | bhūtam:nature); यत् (yat) – which; पुण्डरीकं (puṇḍarīkaṃ) – lotus; पुरमध्यसग्म्स्थम् (puramadhyasagmstham) – situated in the city center (pura:city | madhya:center | saṃstham:situated); तत्रापि (tatrāpi) – even there; दहं (dahaṃ) – small; गगनं (gaganaṃ) – sky; विशोक (viśoka) – free from sorrow; तस्मिन् (tasmin) – in that; यदन्तस्तदुपासितव्यम् (yadantastadupāsitavyam) – that which is inside should be meditated upon (yat:which | antas:inside | tat:that | upāsitavyam:should be meditated upon);

contentBlocks.original:
यो वेदादौ स्वरः प्रोक्तो
वेदान्ते च प्रतिष्ठितः।
तस्य प्रकृतिलीनस्य
यः परः स महेश्वरः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yo vedadau svarah prokto
vedante ca pratiṣṭhitah
tasya prakrtilinasya
yah parah sa maheśvarah.
contentBlocks.translation: The one who is the syllable (om) that is uttered at the beginning of the Veda and is well- established in the upanişads, the one who is the cause, the truth of that causal principle which is resolved (in himself), he is the Lord, Maheshvara.
contentBlocks.meanings: यः (yaḥ) – who; वेदादौ (vedādau) – at the beginning of Veda (veda:Veda | ādau:at the beginning); स्वरः (svaraḥ) – syllable; प्रोक्तः (proktaḥ) – uttered; वेदान्ते (vedānte) – in the Upanishads (veda:Veda | ante:end, Upanishads); च (ca) – and; प्रतिष्ठितः (pratiṣṭhitaḥ) – established; तस्य (tasya) – of that; प्रकृतिलीनस्य (prakṛtilīnasya) – resolved in nature (prakṛti:nature | līnasya:resolved in); यः (yaḥ) – who; परः (paraḥ) – beyond; स (sa) – he; महेश्वरः (maheśvaraḥ) – the great Lord (mahā:great | īśvaraḥ:Lord);
